title: Sales related occupations n.e.c.
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the selling, buying, leasing, or governance activities of commercial, industrial, or residential real estate properties. Includes managers of homeowner and condominium associations, rented or leased housing units, buildings, or land (including rights-of-way). Represent and promote artists, performers, and athletes in dealings with current or prospective employers. May handle contract negotiation and other business matters for clients. Prepare cost estimates for product manufacturing, construction projects, or services to aid management in bidding on or determining price of product or service. May specialize according to particular service performed or type of product manufactured. (Based on 50 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Plan and prepare work schedules and keep records of employees' work schedules and time cards.
Inventory stock and reorder when inventory drops to a specified level.
Plan budgets and authorize payments and returns for merchandise.
Confer with company officials to develop methods and procedures to increase sales and promote business.
Plan and coordinate advertising campaigns and sales promotions, and prepare merchandise displays and advertising copy.
Review inventory and sales records to prepare reports for management and budgeting.
Examine merchandise to ensure correct pricing and display functionality as advertised.
Estimate consumer demand and determine types and amounts of goods to be sold.
Formulate pricing policies for merchandise based on profitability requirements.
Instruct staff on handling difficult sales situations.
Establish and implement departmental policies, goals, objectives, and procedures.
Perform work activities of subordinates, such as organizing displays and selling merchandise.
Hire, train, and evaluate personnel in sales or marketing establishments, making promotions or dismissals as needed.
Examine products purchased for resale to assess their condition.
Keep records of purchases, sales, and requisitions.
Enforce safety, health, and security rules.
Direct and supervise employees engaged in sales and customer service activities.
Monitor sales activities to ensure customers receive satisfactory service and quality goods.
Provide customer service by assisting clients and addressing inquiries and complaints.
Sell products being promoted and maintain records of sales.
Provide product samples, coupons, and other incentives to encourage sales.
Meet with clients and other advisors to understand their financial goals and circumstances.
Advise clients on financing options and mortgage terms to help them obtain the best rates.
Advise clients on market conditions, prices, and legal requirements regarding property.
Inspect properties to evaluate their suitability before showing them to clients.
Generate lists of properties that match buyers' needs and financial resources using computer systems.
Adjust rent prices based on market research and trends.
Administer first aid to passengers in distress or assist passengers in emergency procedures, such as evacuating a plane following an emergency landing.
Prepare reports to provide answers in response to specific problems and monitor passenger behavior to identify threats to the safety of the crew and other passengers.
Assist customers, such as responding to customer complaints and updating them about back-ordered parts, and answer questions regarding the store and its merchandise.
Greet customers and ascertain what each customer wants or needs, and advise customers on use and care of merchandise.
Keep records of transactions, maintain knowledge of current sales and promotions, and monitor passenger behavior to identify threats to the safety of the crew and other passengers.
Prepare sales slips or sales contracts and compute sales prices, total purchases, and receive and process cash or credit payment.
Meet with supervisor daily to submit completed assignments and discuss progress, and confer with managers to identify trends or key group interests or provide advice on business decisions.
Prepare or review detailed design drawings, specifications, or lists related to solar installations and demonstrate the operation or use of technical or scientific products.
Sell or arrange for delivery, insurance, financing, or service contracts for merchandise, and provide customers with information such as quotes, orders, or sales.
Answer inquiries regarding information such as schedules, accommodations, procedures, or policies, and contact customers to advise them of travel conveyance changes or to confirm reservations.
Negotiate prices or terms of sales or service agreements.
Collaborate with colleagues to exchange information, such as selling strategies or marketing information.
Maintain customer records using automated systems.
Answer customers' questions about product features, benefits, availability, and prices.
Circulate among potential customers or travel by foot, truck, or other means to deliver or sell merchandise or services.
Develop prospect lists and identify prospective customers using directories, leads, or trade shows.
Distribute product samples or literature detailing products or services, and provide samples and catalogs to customers.
Prepare sales presentations or proposals to explain product specifications or applications.
Provide ongoing technical support to customers and resolve problems after sales or contract signings.
Prepare sales contracts, order forms, and necessary paperwork for orders.
Evaluate market conditions, product innovations, and competitors' services, prices, and sales.
Contact prospective customers, discuss how services or products can meet their needs, and persuade them to make purchases.
Monitor stock levels, reorder merchandise as necessary, and stock carts or stands.
Negotiate details of contracts, payments, and with retail merchants to improve product exposure.
Prepare financial plans and reports, compute costs, and provide information on product delivery schedules and contracts.
Present information about the energy efficiency or environmental impact of products to customers.
Attend sales or trade meetings to learn about market conditions, regulations, and business trends.
Compute and compare costs of services and negotiate prices or contracts for securities or commodities.
Prepare and send requests for price quotations and ensure accuracy of sales orders before processing.
Make arrangements for proper packaging and labeling of exported goods, including customs compliance.
Inform investment decisions by analyzing financial information to forecast business, industry, or economic conditions.
Confer with clients to develop strategies for their careers and explain actions taken on their behalf.
Collect fees, commissions, or other payments according to contract terms.
Develop contacts with individuals and organizations, applying strategies to ensure clients' success.
Schedule promotional or performance engagements for clients and manage their business affairs like travel and marketing.
Negotiate with managers, promoters, and union officials regarding clients' contractual rights and obligations.
Conduct audits or interviews to evaluate potential clients and assess industry trends and deals.
Prepare and deliver sales presentations to customers to protect and increase advertising and develop new accounts.
Consult with clients and vendors to discuss estimates and resolve issues concerning projects.
Prepare periodic accounting statements and advise clients on financial and legal matters.
Inspect outgoing work for compliance with specifications and obtain feedback from customers.
Compile data from records to prepare periodic reports and maintain information on clients and vendors.
Monitor or track sustainability indicators, analyze impacts, and develop reports on sustainability initiatives.
Create marketing media and draft descriptions of merchandise for online posting or sales.
Evaluate costs and prepare estimates for projects, including vendor selection and resource requirements.
Respond to emergency situations and assist clients with day-to-day business matters.
Establish, maintain, or test call trees to ensure appropriate communication during disaster.
Interpret government regulations and applicable codes to ensure compliance and maintain industry standards.
Review design drawings or technical documents for completeness, correctness, or appropriateness.
Provide system design and integration recommendations and assess the nature and level of security threats.
Correspond with online customers via electronic mail or other electronic messaging to address questions or complaints about products.
Develop or revise business plans for online business, focusing on product line, pricing, inventory, or marketing strategy.
Measure and analyze website usage data to maximize search engine returns and enhance user experience.
Order or purchase merchandise to maintain optimal inventory levels and keep inventory of shipping supplies.
Determine and set product prices, and compute or estimate cash, payroll, or personnel requirements.
Monitor sales staff performance to ensure goals are met and provide staff with assistance in performing difficult duties.
Collaborate with search engine shopping specialists to place marketing content in desired online locations to enhance visibility.
Listen to and resolve customer complaints regarding services, products, or personnel to improve customer satisfaction.
Develop emergency management plans for recovery decision making and continuity of operation.
Explain and demonstrate work tasks to new workers and assign training tasks to experienced workers.
Negotiate with project stakeholders or suppliers to obtain resources or materials necessary for operational success.
Inspect materials, stock, vehicles, or equipment to ensure safety, compliance with specifications, and prevent defects.
Conduct security audits to identify potential vulnerabilities and recommend improvements in policies or procedures.
Prepare or organize online storefront marketing materials, optimizing content for search engine criteria.
Develop conceptual designs of security systems and establish requirements for their implementation.
Examine products purchased for resale to determine their condition and ensure accurate pricing and display.
Hire, train, and evaluate personnel to improve team effectiveness and performance.
